title: Interpret Control Availability and Status Indicators on the Visu_MCP_Dual Screen
summary: Use the documented Visu_MCP_Dual screen indicators to determine which controls are active and to review alarms or informational messages shown on the primary operator screen.
when_to_use: Use when viewing the primary operator station HMI screen to determine which Visu_MCP_Dual controls are currently active and to read alarms or informational messages shown in the banner.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a corrective action procedure.
Do not use this runbook to perform fault recovery beyond interpreting the documented screen elements.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: View the Visu_MCP_Dual screen, identify active controls using the green bars above buttons, review banner messages, and record the observed current state.

safety_notes: This runbook supports interpretation only and does not provide corrective actions.
Escalate if the observed control state or message display cannot be matched to the documented screen elements.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI
Visual access to button status indicators

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The operator station HMI is available for viewing.
The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is displayed or can be navigated to.

visual_references.description: Primary operator HMI screen for the tipper machine control panel, including active-control indicators and informational banner.
Operator station display controls showing how to access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen
steps.purpose: Access the primary operator station screen used for control-status interpretation.
steps.instruction: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen on the operator station HMI.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is visible on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The primary operator station control panel screen is displayed and its controls and banner are visible.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be displayed or the operator cannot confirm that the correct screen is open.

actions.label: Open Visu_MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen-navigation controls showing access to F3 "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.
Overall layout of the Operator Station Control Panel for the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be located or opened.
The displayed screen does not match the documented Operator Station Control Panel layout.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: F3 maps to "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.
The Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual" screen) is the primary screen that station operators will need to use.

steps.title: Identify active controls using the green bars
steps.purpose: Determine which buttons are currently active on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
steps.instruction: Look above each button and identify whether the bar above the button is illuminated green to determine which buttons are active.
steps.expected_result: The operator can distinguish active buttons from inactive buttons based on the green illuminated bars.
steps.healthy_condition: Buttons that are active have green illuminated bars above them.
steps.failure_condition: The button indicators are unclear, not visible, or do not match the documented behavior.

actions.label: Inspect button status bars
actions.exact_action: Look above each button and identify whether the bar above the button is illuminated green to determine which buttons are active.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Buttons on the primary operator HMI screen and the bars above them that illuminate green when active.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed control state cannot be matched to the documented screen elements.
The green-bar indication is not visible or does not correspond to the displayed controls.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The bars above the buttons illuminate green to indicate which buttons are active.

steps.title: Read the informational banner
steps.purpose: Review the current alarm or informational message shown on the screen.
steps.instruction: Locate the informational banner and read any displayed alarm or informational message.
steps.expected_result: The current banner message is identified and understood as an alarm or informational message.
steps.healthy_condition: The informational banner is visible and its current message can be read.
steps.failure_condition: The banner is not visible, the message cannot be read, or the displayed content cannot be matched to the documented banner function.

actions.label: Inspect informational banner
actions.exact_action: Locate the informational banner and read any displayed alarm or informational message.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Informational banner area used to display alarms or informational messages.

stop_or_escalate_if: The banner message cannot be read.
The displayed message area does not match the documented informational banner.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages.

steps.title: Navigate additional banner messages
steps.purpose: Review all available messages when more than one message is present in the banner.
steps.instruction: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through additional messages if more than one message is present.
steps.expected_result: Additional banner messages can be viewed one at a time using the banner arrows.
steps.healthy_condition: The right-side banner arrows allow navigation through available messages.
steps.failure_condition: The arrows do not allow message navigation or additional messages cannot be reviewed when present.

actions.label: Navigate banner messages
actions.exact_action: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through messages in the banner.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Arrows on the right side of the informational banner used to move through messages.

stop_or_escalate_if: More than one message appears to be present but the arrows do not allow navigation.
The message navigation controls cannot be matched to the documented banner arrows.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner.

steps.title: Record the observed control and message state
steps.purpose: Capture the current HMI state for operational awareness or follow-up.
steps.instruction: Record the active controls and the displayed message text for the current system state.
steps.expected_result: A record exists of which controls were active and what message text was displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed active controls and banner message text are documented clearly.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot confidently document the active controls or the displayed message text.

actions.label: Document observed state
actions.exact_action: Record the active controls and the displayed message text for the current system state.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Control buttons, green active-status bars, and informational banner text to capture the current screen state.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed control state or message display cannot be matched to the documented screen elements.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The bars above the buttons illuminate green to indicate which buttons are active. An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages.

success_criteria: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is opened and identified correctly.
Active controls are identified using the green illuminated bars above the buttons.
The current alarm or informational banner message is read.
Additional messages are reviewed using the banner arrows when present.
The active controls and displayed message text are recorded for the current system state.
healthy_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen matches the documented Operator Station Control Panel layout.
Green illuminated bars above buttons clearly indicate active controls.
The informational banner is visible and readable.
Banner arrows on the right side allow navigation through messages when more than one message is present.
failure_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be opened or confirmed.
The control-state indicators cannot be interpreted from the screen.
The informational banner or its message text cannot be read.
The observed control state or message display cannot be matched to the documented screen elements.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed control state or message display cannot be matched to the documented screen elements.
Escalate if the Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed or does not match the documented layout.
This runbook supports interpretation only and does not provide corrective actions beyond the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary operator HMI screen layout, green active-status bars above buttons, informational banner, and banner navigation arrows.
Display controls and F3 mapping used to access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The bars above the buttons illuminate green to indicate which buttons are active.
An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages. The arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide a time estimate for this procedure.
The source packet does not specify whether production stop is required.
The source packet does not specify whether lockout/tagout is required.
The source packet does not provide a prescribed recording location or format for documenting the observed state.
